slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series. 

WISH is ProtaTECH’s all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.  
In this module, we’ll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

slides.instructions: Video of scheduling site entered as the URL, and the home page on screen.
slides.text: WISH is available online at wish.schedulingsite.com.

slides.instructions: Video of WISH home page
slides.text:
To access WISH, you will need login credentials. After you complete your assigned WISH training modules, your login details will be sent to the email address listed in your Employee Self Service account. 

Before logging in for the first time, you will need to review and accept the cookies and privacy policy.

slides.instructions: Video of ESS Home page, then back to WISH.
slides.text:
When you were hired, you should have received an email from the Employee Self Service Portal (ESS). This application allows you to access your job trainings, view your pay stubs, request schedules. You should already have an ESS account. ESS can be accessed online at ess.schedulingsite.com.

Your WISH login credentials will be sent to the email address listed on your ESS account.Once you receive your credentials, you can log into WISH and begin completing your work assignments using the knowledge you’ve gained.

slides.slide_name: Do Not Share Your Login Credentials
slides.instructions: Show video of Home page, cookies accepted, iD and password submitted and "Your Name" in the upper right corner of the page.
slides.text:
It is important that you do NOT share your login credentials with anyone. All actions performed using your credentials will be recorded in the system as being completed by you. Some tasks may require you to complete multiple training sessions before you can perform them. You will not be able to complete certain work duties until all assigned training is finished. Your system access and available features are determined by your assigned and completed training.

You will have access to all menus necessary for your role. If you attempt to open a menu for which you have not completed the required training, access will be restricted. If you believe you need access to additional menus, please contact your administrator to arrange the necessary training and obtain the appropriate permissions for your position.

slides.instructions: Video of envelope and message in Broadcast Messages screen.
slides.text: When you log in, you will see your name in the top right corner of the page. On your first login, an envelope icon will appear next to your name. You will need to click on the envelope to open your message. This will direct you to the Broadcast Messages screen. Periodic updates will be posted here to inform of system changes, new features, or scheduled downtime for WISH system updates. You can read the message, mark it as read, or delete messages. Broadcast Messages present one-way communications which cannot be replied to. They are not emails. They are a way for WISH to communicate with you.

slides.instructions: Video of mouse clicking on "Name" to show three menu option available to user.
slides.text: Your name, displayed in the top right corner, offers three menu options when clicked. You can click on My Messages to navigate to the Broadcast Messages screen. Log out of your session by clicking Log Out, and change your password by clicking on the Change Password menu. We recommend changing your password regularly. Changing your password in WISH only updates your password in the WISH application.

The name WISH stands for Workforce Information Systems Hosted, and the application is designed to embody the principles behind this acronym. Whether implemented in part or in full, WISH provides flexible solutions tailored to organizational needs. Key modules—including Scheduling, Billing, Human Resources, and Payroll—are consolidated into a single, integrated platform. By bringing these essential functions together, WISH streamlines workforce management and offers accessible, web-based services from any location with an internet connection.
In the following slides we will explore WISH’s application menus. Each menu item is designed to help you efficiently manage your responsibilities within the system.

The Admin menu provides access to certain system functions, including creating new companies and branches, and granting users access based on their completed training. This menu is restricted to developers only. If you attempt to access a menu item without the necessary permissions, you will receive a message indicating you do not have access to that page. This applies throughout the system wherever permissions are required.

The Billing menu allows you to generate invoices for clients after your branch has completed a job. Whenever a schedule is saved in the system, it is stored for future billing purposes. This ensures you can easily create and provide accurate bills to clients for the events your team has worked.
In the Inventory menu, you can track important items used at events—like radios, jackets, and other supplies.
The Records menu is where you will spend much of your time entering, editing, and updating essential data. Maintaining accurate records is vital for your branch’s operations.

The Reports menu provides access to important documents, such as alpha rosters, sign-in and sign-out sheets for events, Payroll and Billing repors, and employee work schedules. These reports help support job administration and event management.

The Scheduling menu is used by your branch scheduling department. The scheduling department schedules employees to jobs in one of two ways: Schedule by Employee or Schedule by Job. You can self-schedule through your ESS app, which is a different way from what you see here in WISH.
The Payroll menu is where payroll staff review and approve employees’ work hours after an event. This ensures that everyone is properly compensated for their time.

The Time Clock menu is used at event locations where computers are set up for employees to sign in at the start of their shifts and sign out at the end of their shifts. This feature helps accurately track work hours for payroll and attendance purposes.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series. 
WISH is ProtaTECH’s all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.  
In this module, we’ll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

In this module, we'll cover the following Record menu's:
Manage Job
Manage Clients
Manage Contracts
Manage Events 
Manage Venues

Before creating anything in WISH, understand the hierarchy — the order records must exist.

At the top: Company and Branch (already created — CSC and all locations are already in WISH).

Below that:
1. Event — must exist first
2. Job — created under an Event
3. Shift — created under a Job
4. Role — created under a Shift

Nothing can exist without the level above it. You cannot create a Job without an Event. When deleting, work in reverse order from the bottom up.

slides.instructions: Video going through the Add/Edit Event menu,Manage Event.
slides.text: Create an event, add an event name, explain start and end date. Load in date, date where branch moves into event. Load out date, moving out of event. Satisfy the asterisks and Save the record, receive your confirmation and review that clicking save generates an event number and adds new tabs to the menu.

New tabs appear after event is saved. Tabs are added for billing purposes. Clients tab, we can add an existing client directly from here or we can go to the Manage Client menu and add new clients. Once you find the client make sure their status is checked and add them, you can have mulitiple clients in the same event that you need to bill for.

The same thing we did for the clients, we are going to do for the venue. We are going to select and add an existing venue to our event. A well established venue may have all venues already created so you should search for the venue to see if it is already in the system. Only create after checking. An event can be taking place at multiple venues, if this is the case make sure to select all the venues where your event is taking place.

Under the Event, we will always have our Job. Look at the Start and End Date between our Jobs, there is a 15 day span. Click on Add New to create a new Job. Look at asterisks to identify what is needed to create a Job. Job Name, Job Class-important for payroll to knwo how the job is classified. Branch Name should be your Branch Name, Event should be the Event name you created in the Manage Event menu. Venue and Client information should provide the selections you chose in the Manage Event record. If this information does not pop-up, then you not have saved the data in the Event record or you may have added the wrong venue and client.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti
Arrow/progress bar
slides.text: Proceed to next module (if applicable), refer to Quick Reference Guide, contact WISH Administrator for support.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series. 
WISH is ProtaTECH’s all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.  
In this module, we’ll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

In this module, you will learn:
• How to create a Job
• How to modify an existing Job
• How to copy a Job
• How to delete a Job (and why order matters)

Note: If you have Record Maintenance access, this module is included within that permission. Manage Job is for users who create and modify jobs only.

slides.instructions: Screen recording: Records → Manage Job. Click Add New. Fill required fields. Save. Show green confirmation and job number.
slides.text:
Go to Records → Manage Job. Use the filter page to search for existing jobs by name or job number. To create a new job, click Add New.

Fill in all asterisk-marked fields:
• Job Name
• Job Class — use Events for jobs you will schedule employees for and bill a client; Office for in-office hours; Training for training shifts
• Branch Name — auto-fills for you
• Event — select the event from the dropdown
• Venue and Client — these auto-populate from the event
• Start and End Date/Time — use military time format

After saving, copy the job number to your clipboard — you will use it throughout the system.

slides.instructions: Screen recording: Click Shifts tab. Add New Shift. Set name and times. Add Role. Set min/max. Save.
slides.text:
After saving the job, click the Shifts tab. Click Add New Shift. Give it a name — such as "Staff One Call" — and set the start and end time within the job's time window.

Shift times must fall within the job's start and end time. If you go outside that range, WISH will warn you.

Once the shift is saved, click Add Role. Select the role, set your minimum (the client's required number) and maximum (your buffer — always leave wiggle room above the minimum). Save.

You can add multiple shifts and multiple roles under each shift.

slides.instructions: Screen recording: Manage Job search results. Click copy icon. Open copy settings. Select Daily. Set dates. Click Copy.
slides.text:
From Manage Job search results, click the copy icon for the job you want to copy. Open the copy settings link.

Choose from:
• Daily — copies the job to specific dates
• Weekly — repeats on the same days of the week over a date range
• Monthly — copies for every day of the month

Daily is the most commonly used option. Always double-check your start and end dates before confirming — do not include the original date again. Copied jobs receive new unique job numbers.

If a job is canceled, update the Job Class to "Canceled" immediately. This is critical for accounting — open invoices and pending payments depend on accurate information.

To fully delete a job, work in reverse order: delete roles first, then shifts, then the job itself. WISH will not let you delete a job if shifts exist, and will not let you delete a shift if roles exist.

If employees are already scheduled, remove them before deleting the role. Most branches leave canceled jobs in the system and simply update the Job Class.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti
slides.text:
When creating a shift, you can share it with other branches using the Select Branch field. Once saved, those branches can see that shift and schedule their own employees to it.

This is useful for large events where staff from multiple locations are needed. You can even rename the shift to identify the contributing branch — for example, "San Diego Staff."

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti animation
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed this portion of the WISH training. WISH is here to make workforce management easier, and you're now ready to use it with confidence.

Depending on your role and access level, there may be additional training videos. Each video provides the knowledge needed for your tools.

Thank you for taking the time to learn how WISH supports staff management.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), refer to Quick Reference Guide, contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series. 
WISH is ProtaTECH’s all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.  
In this module, we’ll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

slides.instructions: Calendar/web icon
slides.text:
In this module, you will learn how to:
• Publish job shifts to the web so employees can apply
• Enable auto-scheduling for large events
• Review and approve or deny employee shift applications
• Change the Max number of staff that can apply to Shifts
MSS is the manager's side of the scheduling portal. It controls what employees can see and self-schedule for in their ESS app.

slides.instructions: Tools menu → Manager Portal. Show event list and variance column.
slides.text:
Go to the Tools menu in the top navigation and select Manager Portal.

You will see a list of upcoming events for your branch. The variance column shows the difference between employees you have scheduled and the minimum required for each shift.

slides.instructions: Click Web Shift tab. Find event. Check auto-schedule or select individually. Click Add to Web Shift. Show green check appearing.
slides.text:
Click the Web Shift tab. Shifts with an unchecked box on the left are not published yet.

To enable auto-scheduling — where employees can apply without needing your approval — check the Auto Schedule box at the top (selects all jobs) or check individually.

Click Add to Web Shift. A green checkmark confirms employees can now see and self-schedule for the shift.

To disable publishing, click the green checkmark and confirm.

You have two options for how employees get scheduled through ESS:

Auto-Schedule — Employees who apply are automatically accepted on a first-come, first-served basis until the maximum headcount is reached. No action required from you. Ideal for large events.

Manual Approval — Employees apply and you review each application individually. Gives you more control but takes significantly more time.

Most high-volume branches use auto-scheduling for large events.

slides.instructions: Web Shift Approval tab. Set date range. Show All Published Shifts. Expand event. Show employees listed. Approve/Deny buttons.
slides.text:
The Web Shift Approval tab shows applications for published shifts.

Set your date range and click Show All Published Shifts. Shifts with pending activity show small breadcrumb indicators.

Open a shift role to see applicants. Approve or deny each one. The system enforces your headcount limits — it will not let you approve more than the maximum allows.

If you approve one applicant for a full role, go back and deny others who applied.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed this portion of the WISH training. WISH is here to make workforce management easier, and you're now ready to use it with confidence.

Depending on your role and access level, there may be additional training videos. Each video provides the knowledge needed for your tools.

Thank you for taking the time to learn how WISH supports staff management.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), refer to Quick Reference Guide, contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series. 
WISH is ProtaTECH’s all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.  
In this module, we’ll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

WISH offers two manual scheduling methods found under the Scheduling menu:

Schedule by Employee — Used when working with one employee at a time, such as on the phone. You select the employee and assign them to multiple days or shifts.

Schedule by Job — Used to schedule multiple employees at once to the same role and shift. Ideal for bulk scheduling from a list.

In this module, you will learn both methods.

slides.instructions: Scheduling → Schedule by Employee. Show calendar grid. Search employee. Click job. Select shift and role. Click green checkmark. Save.
slides.text:
Go to Scheduling → Schedule by Employee.

The calendar defaults to Saturday through Friday — keep this default so it matches what employees see on their end.

Search for your employee by number or name. Once found, click on the job in the calendar grid. Select the shift and role, then click the green checkmark. Hours will populate on the calendar.

Click Save Schedule when ready. You can schedule the same employee across multiple days before saving.

To remove an employee, click the role on the calendar and delete them, or bring them up and click in their scheduled area — a dialogue box will ask for a reason code.

slides.instructions: Scheduling → Schedule by Job. Paste job number. Check employee roles box. Select role. Search. Select multiple employees. Click Save.
slides.text:
Go to Scheduling → Schedule by Job. Search for the job.

Before searching for employees, check the Check Employee Roles box. This filters results to only show employees who have an active pay rate for the role you are filling.

Select the role, run the search. Employees ready to be scheduled appear in green. Already-scheduled employees appear in red.

Click the names of all employees you want to schedule, then click Save. They will all be assigned at once.

A few things to know:

• Red calendar highlights may indicate overtime — informational only, it will not prevent saving.

• You can set unavailability for an employee on vacation — the calendar shows red across those days.

• You can send a copy of the schedule directly to the employee from this screen before ending a call.

• The scheduling comments button (asterisk icon) lets you add notes that appear on sign-in sheets — for example, flagging an employee who will be late.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed this portion of the WISH training. WISH is here to make workforce management easier, and you're now ready to use it with confidence.

Depending on your role and access level, there may be additional training videos. Each video provides the knowledge needed for your tools.

Thank you for taking the time to learn how WISH supports staff management.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), refer to Quick Reference Guide, contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series. 
WISH is ProtaTECH’s all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.  
In this module, we’ll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.
In this module, you will learn how to use the administrative scheduling screen — which allows you to override standard scheduling restrictions.

Schedule by Job Admin works exactly like Schedule by Job with one key difference: it bypasses certain restrictions.

With admin scheduling, you can:
• Double-book an employee who is already scheduled for another shift at the same time
• Schedule an employee who has exceeded their weekly hour limit
• Override overlap warnings when necessary

This access gives managers flexibility in high-pressure situations such as last-minute event changes.

slides.instructions: Scheduling → Schedule by Job Admin. Search for job. Select role. Show already-scheduled employees with checkboxes still available. Select one. Show overlap warning. Confirm and save.
slides.text:
Go to Scheduling → Schedule by Job Admin. This screen looks identical to Schedule by Job, but employees already scheduled — shown in red on the standard page — still have their checkboxes available here.

If you select an already-scheduled employee and save, WISH displays an overlap warning. You can choose to bypass this warning and proceed.

Use this carefully. Always verify the employee is actually available before proceeding.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed this portion of the WISH training. WISH is here to make workforce management easier, and you're now ready to use it with confidence.

Depending on your role and access level, there may be additional training videos. Each video provides the knowledge needed for your tools.

Thank you for taking the time to learn how WISH supports staff management.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
