slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text: Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series.

slides.instructions: Click Reports in the top navigation. Show all three sub-menus expanding: Summary Roster, Detailed Roster, Job Administration.
slides.text:
With General Reporting access, you have access to the full Reports menu in WISH.

The Reports menu has three sections:
• Job Administration — your daily reports. Alpha rosters, sign-in sheets, schedules, and post-event follow-up.
• Summary Roster — high-level overviews across employees, jobs, clients, venues, and branches.
• Detailed Roster — deeper employee-level reports covering timesheets, licensing, sign-in activity, and availability.
 
We'll cover each one so you know where to go when you need something.

slides.instructions: Reports → Job Administration. Show the full report list on the right side. Scroll through so all report names are visible.
slides.text:
Job Administration is where you'll spend most of your time in the Reports menu. These are the reports that support your day-to-day event operations.

The reports you'll use most often:
• Alpha Roster — who is scheduled for a job, in alphabetical order
• Sign-In / Sign-Out Sheets — paper backup when electronic sign-in isn't available
• Employee Not Sign-In — after an event, who was scheduled but didn't sign in electronically

slides.instructions: Reports → Job Administration → Alpha Roster. Enter Job#, Start Time, Branch. Click Retrieve. Show alphabetical list with shift and role columns.
slides.text:
The Alpha Roster is your pre-event checklist — an alphabetical list of every employee scheduled for a job.

To run it:
1. Go to Reports → Job Administration → Alpha Roster
2. Enter the Job Number, Start Time (the event date), and your Branch
3. Click Retrieve

You'll see each employee's name, their assigned shift, and their role. Bring this with you to the event as your on-site reference.

slides.instructions: Reports → Job Administration → Sign-In Sign-Out Sheets. Enter Job#, Start Time, Branch. Click Retrieve. Show the printable sheet.
slides.text:
When electronic sign-in isn't set up at an event location, you'll use a paper sign-in sheet.

To generate one:
1. Go to Reports → Job Administration → Sign-In Sign-Out Sheets
2. Enter the Job Number, Start Time, and Branch
3. Click Retrieve and print the sheet

Employees sign in and out by hand. After the event, those times get entered manually in Manage Process Hours.

Always print this before you leave for the event. If electronic sign-in goes down on-site, this is your backup.

slides.instructions: Reports → Job Administration → Employee Not Sign-In. Enter Job# and date. Click Retrieve. Show list of employees with no recorded sign-in.
slides.text:
After an event, Employee Not Sign-In tells you which scheduled employees have no electronic sign-in on record.

To run it:
1. Go to Reports → Job Administration → Employee Not Sign-In
2. Enter the Job Number and the event date
3. Click Retrieve

For each employee on the list, determine whether they actually worked. If they did, their hours need to be entered manually in Manage Process Hours. If they didn't show up, mark them as a no-show in the system.

slides.instructions: Reports → Summary Roster. Show the dropdown list of report options: Employee Summary, Workgroup Summary, Job Summary, Client Summary, Venue Summary, Branch Summary, Background Check.
slides.text:
The Summary Roster section gives you high-level overviews — useful when you need a broad picture rather than a specific job's details.

Reports available here include summaries organized by employee, workgroup, job, client, venue, and branch. There's also a Background Check status overview.

These reports are most useful for reviewing patterns across your branch — like checking which employees are active, or getting an overview of all jobs in a date range.

For descriptions of each report, refer to your Quick Reference Guide.

slides.instructions: Reports → Detailed Roster. Show the dropdown list of report options. Highlight Employee Time Sheet Details, Employee Sign-In Sign-Out Details, Employee Licensing Report.
slides.text:
The Detailed Roster section goes deeper — employee-level reports with specifics on time, licensing, availability, and training.

Reports you may find useful here:
• Employee Time Sheet Details — full timesheet data for an employee over a date range
• Employee Sign-In Sign-Out Details — exact timestamps for sign-in and sign-out activity
• Employee Licensing Report — guard card and licensing status across your employee pool
• Available Employees Report — who is eligible and available to be scheduled
• JobTraining Report — which WISH training modules each employee has completed

For the full list of Detailed Roster reports, refer to your Quick Reference Guide.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti animation
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed the General Reporting module.

You now know how to navigate all three sections of the Reports menu and how to run the reports you'll use most on the job.
 
If you're ever unsure which report to use, your administrator can help point you in the right direction.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable)

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series.

WISH is ProtaTECH's all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.

In this module, we'll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

slides.instructions: Click Reports in the top navigation. Show the full menu — Summary Roster, Detailed Roster, Job Administration, Payroll Reports. Highlight Payroll Reports.
slides.text:
With Payroll Reporting access, you have everything in General Reporting plus one additional section — Payroll Reports.

Payroll Reports lives in the Reports menu alongside Summary Roster, Detailed Roster, and Job Administration. This is where you go to verify employee hours and pay before payroll is finalized.

Think of these reports as your review tools — you run them to make sure what's in the system matches what actually happened at the event before payroll gets processed.

slides.instructions: Reports → Payroll Reports. Show the full list of available reports: Employee Time Register, Employee Time Register (Break), Emp Time Register With Notes, Financial Analysis Report, Export PerDiem Report, Manage Temp Cost Code Number, Employee Estimate Hours.
slides.text:
Go to Reports → Payroll Reports to access this section.

Reports available here:
• Employee Time Register — the primary report. Shows each employee's hours worked by job and shift.
• Employee Time Register (Break) — same as above, with break time broken out separately.
• Emp Time Register With Notes — includes any notes attached to an employee's timesheet entry.
• Financial Analysis Report — a cost-facing view of hours and pay across jobs.
• Export PerDiem Report — exports per diem payments for payroll processing.
• Manage Temp Cost Code Number — for branches that use cost codes to categorize labor.
• Employee Estimate Hours — projected hours based on scheduled shifts before actuals are entered.

For most day-to-day payroll review, you'll be working with the Employee Time Register.

slides.instructions: Reports → Payroll Reports → Employee Time Register. Enter date range and Branch. Click Retrieve. Show report with employee names, job, shift times, regular hours, and overtime.
slides.text:
The Employee Time Register is your go-to report before payroll is finalized. It shows you what hours are on record for each employee across a selected date range.

To run it:
1. Go to Reports → Payroll Reports → Employee Time Register
2. Enter your date range and confirm your Branch
3. Click Retrieve

The report shows each employee's name, the job they worked, their sign-in and sign-out times, regular hours, and any overtime.

Review this carefully before payroll is processed. If something looks off — wrong times, missing entries, unexpected overtime — go back to Manage Process Hours and make the correction before approving.

slides.instructions: Show Employee Time Register (Break) report output side by side with standard Time Register. Point out the break column. Then briefly show Emp Time Register With Notes.
slides.text:
Two variations of the Employee Time Register are available depending on what you need to see:

Employee Time Register (Break) — Use this when employees took scheduled breaks that need to be accounted for separately. The report adds a break time column so you can verify break duration against what was entered.

Emp Time Register With Notes — Use this when you need to see any notes that were attached during the sign-in or hour approval process. Useful when there were corrections, late arrivals, or discrepancies that someone flagged at the time.

For most standard reviews, the base Employee Time Register is sufficient. Pull a variant when you need that extra layer of detail.

Here's when payroll reports fit into your workflow:

Before the event:
• Employee Estimate Hours — run this to see projected hours based on the schedule. Useful for labor cost planning.

After the event, before payroll is finalized:
• Employee Time Register — verify that all hours were captured correctly and match what employees actually worked.
• Employee Time Register (Break) or With Notes — pull these if there were corrections or anything flagged during sign-in.
• Financial Analysis Report — review cost totals by job if you need to reconcile against a budget.

Important: Always complete your review in Payroll Reports before payroll is approved. Once hours are finalized, corrections become significantly more difficult.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti animation
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed the Payroll Reporting module.

You now know how to access Payroll Reports, run the Employee Time Register, and use payroll data to verify hours before payroll is finalized.

If anything looks off in a report, contact your administrator before payroll is approved — it's always easier to correct before finalization than after.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series.

In this module, we'll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

slides.instructions: Click Reports in the top navigation. Show the full menu with all six sections visible: Summary Roster, Detailed Roster, Job Administration, Payroll Reports, Billing Reports, Manager Reports.
slides.text:
Admin Reporting gives you access to the complete Reports menu in WISH — all six sections.

You have everything from General Reporting and Payroll Reporting, plus two additional sections that are exclusive to this permission level:
• Billing Reports — invoice and cost reports for client billing oversight
• Manager Reports — credential, scheduling efficiency, and MVR reports for branch management

As an admin-level user, you have a full view of event operations from scheduling through billing. These reports support oversight, auditing, and decision-making across your branch.

slides.instructions: Briefly hover over Summary Roster, Detailed Roster, Job Administration, Payroll Reports to show they are all still there.
slides.text:
The first four sections of the Reports menu work the same way they do in General Reporting and Payroll Reporting:

• Job Administration — alpha rosters, sign-in sheets, employee schedules, post-event follow-up
• Summary Roster — high-level overviews by employee, job, client, venue, and branch
• Detailed Roster — employee-level detail on timesheets, licensing, availability, and training
• Payroll Reports — Employee Time Register and related reports for verifying hours before payroll is finalized

If you haven't completed the General Reporting and Payroll Reporting modules, review those first. This module focuses on the two sections that are new to you: Billing Reports and Manager Reports.

slides.instructions: Reports → Billing Reports. Show the full list: Billing Invoice Ledger Report, Payroll UnBilled Hour Report, Labour Cost Estimation, Billing Invoice Register, Billing Ledger Details.
slides.text:
Billing Reports give you visibility into the financial side of your events — what has been invoiced, what hasn't, and what labor costs look like.

Reports available here:
• Billing Invoice Ledger Report — a record of all invoices generated, with amounts and client details
• Payroll UnBilled Hour Report — shows approved hours that have not yet been billed to a client. Use this to make sure nothing falls through the cracks before closing out an event.
• Labour Cost Estimation — projects labor costs based on scheduled hours and pay rates. Useful for pre-event budget planning.
• Billing Invoice Register — a summary register of all billing activity across a date range
• Billing Ledger Details — a detailed line-by-line view of billing transactions

For most day-to-day use, Payroll UnBilled Hour Report is the one to check regularly — it tells you what work has been done but not yet invoiced.

slides.instructions: Reports → Manager Reports. Show the full list: Schedule Efficiency, Employee Credential Report, Job Credential Report, Employee Task Report, MVR Roster, Branch MVR Roster, Master MVR Roster With Date, Branch MVR Roster Without Date.
slides.text:
Manager Reports cover credential verification, scheduling performance, and motor vehicle record (MVR) tracking — tools for branch-level oversight.

Reports available here:
• Schedule Efficiency — measures how well scheduled headcount matched actual sign-in counts across jobs. Useful for identifying gaps in scheduling accuracy.
• Employee Credential Report — shows credential status (guard cards, licenses) for individual employees
• Job Credential Report — credential requirements and compliance status for a specific job
• Employee Task Report — tracks outstanding onboarding and compliance tasks by employee
• MVR Roster — motor vehicle record status for employees who drive as part of their role
• Branch MVR Roster — MVR status across all applicable employees in your branch
• Master MVR Roster With Date — full MVR roster including check dates
• Branch MVR Roster Without Date — MVR roster view without date filters, for a broad status overview

Credential and MVR reports are especially important before scheduling employees for positions that require specific licensing or driving.

As an Admin Reporting user, your reports touch every phase of event operations:

Before the event:
• Labour Cost Estimation — plan your labor budget
• Employee Credential Report / Job Credential Report — verify employees are licensed for the roles you're filling
• Schedule Efficiency — review past events to improve future scheduling

During and after the event:
• Job Administration reports — alpha rosters, sign-in sheets, no-show follow-up
• Payroll Reports — verify hours before finalizing
• Payroll UnBilled Hour Report — confirm all worked hours are captured for billing

For billing and closeout:
• Billing Invoice Ledger / Billing Invoice Register — confirm invoices have been generated
• Billing Ledger Details — audit-level review of billing transactions

The goal is a clean handoff at every stage: scheduling feeds payroll, payroll feeds billing. Your reports give you the visibility to make sure that chain is intact.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti animation
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed the Admin Reporting module.

You now have a full picture of the WISH Reports menu and how each section supports your branch operations — from scheduling and payroll through billing and compliance.

If you're unsure which report to use for a specific situation, your administrator or the Quick Reference Guide is the best place to start.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series.


In this module, we'll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

slides.slide_name: What Workforce Scheduler Maintenance Gives You
slides.instructions: Navigate to Records → Manage Employee. Show the search screen.
slides.text:
Workforce Scheduler Maintenance gives you access to employee records in WISH — specifically the ability to look up employees and update three things:

• Licensing — guard card numbers and expiration dates
• Background Checks — status and check history
• Employee Notes — branch-level notes on an employee

Everything else in the employee record is view-only for you. You won't be able to change pay rates, status, contact information, or anything outside of those three areas. If you see a field you can't edit, that's by design — those changes require a higher permission level.

slides.instructions: Records → Manage Employee. Show the search filters: Branch Name, Department, WorkGroup, Employee#, First Name, Last Name, Status, SSN last 4 digits. Type a last name. Click Search. Show the results list with Last Name, First Name, Employee#, Branch, Status columns.
slides.text:
Go to Records → Manage Employee to look up an employee.

You can search by:
• Employee number
• First or last name
• Last 4 digits of their SSN — useful if you're not sure of the spelling
• Status — defaults to Active; change this if you're looking for an inactive employee

Leave fields blank to broaden your search, or fill in multiple fields to narrow it down. Click Search when ready.

Your results will show Last Name, First Name, Employee Number, Branch Name, and Status. To open an employee's record, click their last name — it appears as a blue link.

slides.instructions: Click an employee last name link. Show the employee record opening. Point out the tabs across the top of the record. Highlight the tabs that are accessible: Licensing, Background Check, Employee Notes.
slides.text:
Clicking an employee's last name opens their full record. You'll see several tabs across the top.

With Workforce Scheduler Maintenance access, the tabs you can work with are:
• Licensing
• Background Check
• Employee Notes

Other tabs — such as Personal Info, Pay Rate, and Disciplinary Action — are visible but not editable at this permission level. If you need to make changes outside of your three tabs, you'll need to contact someone with higher access.

Always confirm you have the right employee before making any updates — double-check the name and employee number at the top of the record.

slides.instructions: Click the Licensing tab inside an employee record. Show the guard card number field and expiration date. Show how to enter or update a guard card number and expiration date. Click Save.
slides.text:
The Licensing tab is where guard card information is stored for employees who work positions that require a license.

What you can do here:
• View the current guard card number and expiration date
• Add a guard card number if one hasn't been entered yet
• Update the expiration date when a card is renewed

Before scheduling an employee for a licensed position, always check this tab to confirm their guard card is active and not expired. If the card is expired, do not schedule them for a licensed role until the record has been updated with a valid card.

After making any changes, click Save to record them.

slides.instructions: Click the Background Check tab. Show the BG check status field and date. Show updating the status and adding a new check date. Click Save.
slides.text:
The Background Check tab shows the status and history of an employee's background check.

What you can do here:
• View the current background check status
• Update the status when a new check has been completed
• Record the date of the most recent check

This information directly affects whether an employee can be scheduled for certain positions. If a background check is pending or expired, flag it with your administrator before scheduling that employee.

After making any updates, click Save.

slides.instructions: Click the Employee Notes tab. Show existing notes if any. Show typing a new note — include date, what the note is about. Click Save.
slides.text:
The Employee Notes tab is where your branch records important information about an employee that isn't captured elsewhere in the system.

Notes are branch-level — they're visible to anyone at your branch with access to the employee record, but are not shared system-wide.

Common uses for employee notes:
• Flagging a scheduling concern or preference
• Recording follow-up items that need attention
• Noting equipment issued to an employee
• Documenting any informal communications that should be on record

When adding a note, always include the date and a brief description of what the note is about. This makes it easier for others on your team to understand the context later.

Click Save after adding or editing a note.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti animation
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed the Workforce Scheduler Maintenance module.

You now know how to search for employees in WISH, open their records, and update the three areas within your access: Licensing, Background Check, and Employee Notes.

Remember — always verify guard card and background check status before scheduling employees for positions that require them. When in doubt, check with your administrator before scheduling.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series.

In this module, we'll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

slides.slide_name: What Workforce Admin Maintenance Gives You
slides.instructions: Records → Manage Employee. Show the search screen briefly — same as before.
slides.text:
Workforce Admin Maintenance builds on Workforce Scheduler Maintenance. You get to the employee record the same way — Records → Manage Employee, search, click the employee's last name.

At this permission level you can view and edit everything from Workforce Scheduler Maintenance, plus three additional areas:

• Disciplinary Action — view existing records and add new ones
• Pay Rates — view and update employee pay rates
• Training Records — view which WISH training modules an employee has completed

slides.instructions: Open an employee record. Click the Disciplinary Action tab. Show any existing records. Show clicking Add New — fill in date, type, and notes fields. Click Save.
slides.text:
The Disciplinary Action tab is where formal disciplinary records are logged for an employee.

What you can do here:
• View the full history of disciplinary actions on file
• Add a new disciplinary action record

When adding a new record, fill in:
• Date — the date the action occurred
• Type — the category of disciplinary action
• Notes — a clear description of what happened and any actions taken

Be precise and factual when writing notes. These records are part of the employee's permanent file and may be referenced during reviews or investigations.

Click Save after entering the record.

slides.instructions: Click the Pay Rates tab. Show the current active pay rate. Show clicking to deactivate the current rate. Show adding a new rate with the role and dollar amount. Click Save.
slides.text:
The Pay Rates tab shows what an employee is paid per hour for each role they're qualified for.

Important rule: never edit an existing pay rate directly. Changing a rate that's already in the system will alter historical payroll records.

The correct process to update a pay rate:
1. Find the current active rate
2. Deactivate it — this closes it out without deleting the history
3. Add a new rate with the updated amount and an effective start date

If a role is missing from the employee's pay rate list, add it here. An employee cannot be scheduled for a role they don't have an active pay rate for — WISH uses this to filter results in Schedule by Job.

slides.instructions: Click the Training tab inside the employee record. Show the list of WISH training modules and their completion status.
slides.text:
The Training Records tab shows which WISH training modules an employee has been assigned and completed.

What you can do here:
• View training completion status for each module
• Confirm whether an employee has finished the training required for their role

This is useful before scheduling someone for a position that requires specific training. If a module shows as incomplete, the employee may not have the access or knowledge needed to perform that role in WISH.

Training assignments and completions are managed by administrators — if a training record looks incorrect, flag it with your administrator rather than attempting to change it yourself.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti animation
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed the Workforce Admin Maintenance module.

You now know how to access and update Disciplinary Action records, Pay Rates, and Training information within an employee's record.

Remember — always deactivate an existing pay rate before adding a new one. Never edit a rate that's already been used for payroll.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series.

In this module, we'll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

slides.slide_name: What Employee HR Record Maintenance Gives You
slides.instructions: Records → Manage Employee. Show the search screen briefly.
slides.text:
Employee HR Record Maintenance is the broadest level of access to individual employee data in WISH. You get to the employee record the same way as always — Records → Manage Employee, search, click the employee's last name.

At this level you have full access to the employee record, including everything in Workforce Scheduler Maintenance and Workforce Admin Maintenance, plus:

• Personal Info — name, date of birth, status changes
• Contact Info — address, phone number, email address
• Background Checks — full history and status updates
• Shared Branches — which branches can view and schedule this employee
• Exclusions — clients or jobs this employee cannot be assigned to
• Tasks — onboarding and compliance items

Changes made here affect the employee system-wide across all branches. Use this access carefully.

slides.instructions: Open an employee record. Show the Personal Info tab — name, date of birth, status dropdown. Then show the Contact Info tab — address, phone, email fields. Make a small edit and click Save.
slides.text:
Personal Info and Contact Info are the foundation of the employee record.

Personal Info includes:
• Legal name
• Date of birth
• Employee status — Active, Inactive, etc.

Contact Info includes:
• Home address
• Phone number
• Email address — this is the address WISH uses for all Mail By communications and login credential delivery

Keep these fields accurate. An outdated email address means the employee won't receive schedule communications or their login credentials. An incorrect status can prevent them from being scheduled or paid correctly.

Always click Save after making any changes.

slides.instructions: Click the Shared Branches tab. Show existing shared branches if any. Show adding a new branch — select from dropdown, click Add. Show removing a branch.
slides.text:
The Shared Branches tab controls which branches beyond the employee's home branch can see and schedule them.

When an employee is shared to another branch:
• That branch can search for them in Manage Employee
• That branch can schedule them for their jobs
• The employee's record and pay rate information becomes visible to that branch

Common use cases:
• Large events where staff from multiple locations are needed
• Employees who regularly work across multiple branches

Add branches carefully — sharing an employee gives another branch access to their record information. Remove the sharing when it's no longer needed.

slides.instructions: Click the Exclusions tab. Show the client exclusion list. Show adding a client or job exclusion — select from dropdown, save.
slides.text:
The Exclusions tab lets you flag clients or jobs that a specific employee should never be assigned to.

Once an exclusion is set:
• The employee will not appear as available when scheduling for that client or job
• This prevents accidental scheduling conflicts or safety concerns

Common reasons for exclusions:
• A client has requested the employee not return to their venue
• There is a known conflict between the employee and a specific account
• A safety or conduct issue tied to a specific location

Exclusions are not the same as disciplinary action — they're a scheduling control. Document the reason in Employee Notes so the context is on record.

slides.instructions: Records → Manage Employee Status. Show filtering by branch and status. Show selecting multiple employees. Show applying a bulk status change.
slides.text:
In addition to updating individual employee records, Employee HR Record Maintenance gives you access to Manage Employee Status — a tool for updating multiple employees at once.

Go to Records → Manage Employee Status.

Filter by branch and current status to pull a list of employees. Select the ones you want to update, choose the new status, and apply the change to all of them at once.

This is most useful after large events:
• Deactivating seasonal employees who have finished their assignments
• Reactivating employees returning for a new season

Bulk status changes affect every selected employee immediately. Double-check your selection before applying.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti animation
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed the Employee HR Record Maintenance module.

You now have full access to the employee record in WISH. With that comes responsibility — changes you make here affect the employee across all branches and all functions of the system.

When in doubt about a change, check with your administrator before saving. It's always easier to confirm first than to correct after the fact.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series.

WISH is ProtaTECH's all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.

In this module, we'll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

slides.instructions: Show Tools menu. Scroll to Import From Hiring Portal. Click it. Show the Import From Hiring Portal screen load.
slides.text:
Hiring Manager access gives you one specific tool: the ability to import applicants from the hiring portal into WISH.

You'll find it under Tools → Import From Hiring Portal. This is not under the Records menu — go to Tools in the top navigation.

When someone applies and is processed through the hiring portal, they don't automatically exist in WISH. This screen is where you bring them in, review their status, and create their employee record so they can be scheduled for jobs.

slides.instructions: Show the Import From Hiring Portal screen. Point out Branch Name, Employee Type filters. Show the summary count row: Total, Duplicate, Import, Hired, Rehired. Highlight the column headers in the employee list: SSN, Name, Branch, BG Status, E-Verify Status, Status, Hiring Status.
slides.text:
Before importing anyone, understand what you're looking at.

At the top, set your filters:
• Branch Name — select your branch
• Employee Type — Regular Employee in most cases

Click Search to pull the applicant list.

Above the list you'll see a summary row:
• Total — all applicants returned by your search
• Duplicate — applicants already in WISH under a matching SSN
• Import — applicants successfully imported in this session
• Hired — applicants whose hiring status is Hired
• Rehired — returning employees being brought back

The list itself shows each applicant's SSN, name, branch, Background Check status, E-Verify status, current status, and hiring status. Review these columns carefully before taking any action.

slides.instructions: Show an applicant row. Point out BG Status column showing APPROVED and E-Verify Status showing EMPLOYMENTAUTHORIZED. Show the Status column showing Hired.
slides.text:
Before importing an applicant, two things need to be confirmed:

1. Background Check Status — should show APPROVED. Do not import applicants whose background check is still pending or has not cleared.

2. E-Verify Status — should show EMPLOYMENTAUTHORIZED. This confirms the applicant is authorized to work.

Also check the Status column — it should show Hired before you proceed with the import. If an applicant shows a different status, do not import them until their hiring process is complete.

If you see Duplicate in the summary count, that applicant already exists in WISH. Do not import duplicates — importing a duplicate can create record conflicts in the system.

slides.instructions: Click the checkbox next to an approved applicant row. Show the dropdown: Select, Rejected, Rescind. Choose Select. Show the Import Applicant button at the bottom. Click Import Applicant. Show the confirmation.
slides.text:
Once you've confirmed an applicant is cleared and ready:

1. Click the checkbox next to their name — a dropdown will appear with three options:
   • Select — marks the applicant for import
   • Rejected — marks them as rejected, removing them from consideration
   • Rescind — reverses a previous action if needed

2. Choose Select

3. Click Import Applicant at the bottom of the screen

The system will create their employee record in WISH. After a successful import, the applicant will appear in Records → Manage Employee and can be assigned a pay rate and scheduled for jobs.

You can also use CSVExported to download the applicant list to a spreadsheet if you need to share or archive the data.

slides.instructions: Navigate to Records → Manage Employee. Search for the newly imported employee by name. Show their record has been created with personal info populated.
slides.text:
Once an applicant is imported, their basic record is created in WISH using the information from the hiring portal.

Your next steps after importing:
• Go to Records → Manage Employee and search for the new employee to confirm their record was created correctly
• Verify their personal information populated properly — name, SSN, contact details
• Assign their default pay rate if it wasn't set during import — they cannot be scheduled without an active pay rate on file

If anything looks incorrect in the record after import, contact your administrator before scheduling the employee for any jobs.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti animation
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed the Hiring Manager module.

You now know how to access the Import From Hiring Portal screen, review applicant status before importing, and bring new employees into WISH so they can be scheduled for jobs.

Remember — always confirm Background Check and E-Verify status before importing. When in doubt, check with your administrator before proceeding.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series.

In this module, we'll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

slides.instructions: Records menu open. Scroll to the bottom and click Manage Employee Task.
slides.text:
HR Admin access gives you one focused tool: Manage Employee Task.

You'll find it at the bottom of the Records menu — Records → Manage Employee Task.

This screen shows you all employees and whether they have completed or still have pending tasks in WISH. Tasks typically include onboarding requirements, training completions, document submissions, and compliance acknowledgments.

Your job here is to monitor task status across your employee pool and follow up with anyone who has outstanding items.

slides.slide_name: Reading the Manage Employee Task Screen
slides.instructions: Show the Manage Employee Task screen fully loaded. Point out the employee list with columns for name, employee number, status. Zoom in on the task column at the far right — show a checkmark on one row and an X on another.
slides.text:
The Manage Employee Task screen displays your employees in a list with their basic information — name, employee number, and current status.

At the end of each row you'll see the task indicator:
• Checkmark — the employee has completed all assigned tasks. No action needed.
• X — the employee has one or more pending tasks that still need to be completed.

Employees with an X need your attention. They may be missing a required training, a document submission, or another compliance item that needs to be resolved before they can fully perform their role in WISH.

slides.instructions: Click on an employee row that shows an X. Show the task detail that opens — task name, description, due date, and status. Show what a pending task looks like vs a completed one.
slides.text:
When you see an X next to an employee, click on their row to see the details of what's outstanding.

The task detail will show you:
• The name of the task
• A description of what needs to be completed
• The due date if one has been set
• The current status — pending, in progress, or complete

From here you can:
• Review exactly what the employee needs to do
• Follow up with the employee directly to make sure they complete it
• Coordinate with your administrator if a task requires action from the branch side

Task completion itself is typically done by the employee through their ESS account or by an administrator — your role is to monitor and follow up, not to mark tasks complete on their behalf.

Pending tasks aren't just administrative — they can directly affect what an employee can do in the system.

In WISH, certain access and scheduling capabilities are tied to training and task completion. An employee with outstanding tasks may:
• Not yet have their WISH login credentials issued
• Be restricted from accessing menus they need for their role
• Show as incomplete in compliance reporting

For new employees especially, task completion is part of the onboarding process. Staying on top of the Manage Employee Task screen helps make sure new hires get fully set up and can start working without delays.

Make it a habit to check this screen regularly — not just when something seems wrong.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti animation
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed the HR Admin module.

You now know how to access Manage Employee Task, read the task status indicators, and identify employees who have pending items that need follow-up.

Check this screen regularly — keeping tasks current keeps your employees set up correctly and your branch running smoothly.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series.

WISH is ProtaTECH's all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.

In this module, we'll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.

slides.instructions: Click Tools in the top navigation. Show the three Mail By options in the menu: Mail By Employee, Mail By Available Employee, Mail By Job.
slides.text:
Mail By access gives you three communication tools, all found under the Tools menu:

• Mail By Employee — send a message directly to one or more specific employees
• Mail By Available Employee — send a message to employees who are available but not yet scheduled for a job
• Mail By Job — send a message to all employees already scheduled on a specific job

All three options send messages to the email address on file in each employee's WISH record. Choose the right one based on who you're trying to reach and why.

slides.instructions: Tools → Mail By Employee. Show the employee search field. Search for and select one or more employees. Show the message composition area. Show the Send button.
slides.text:
Use Mail By Employee when you need to reach a specific person or a select group of employees by name.

To send a message:
1. Go to Tools → Mail By Employee
2. Search for the employee by name or employee number
3. Select them from the results — you can select multiple employees
4. Type your subject and message
5. Click Send

Common uses:
• Following up with an individual about a schedule or assignment
• Sending a document or reminder to a specific group
• Direct communication when you know exactly who needs to receive it

slides.instructions: Tools → Mail By Available Employee. Show the filter options — branch, date, role. Show the list of available employees populating. Show selecting all and composing a message.
slides.text:
Use Mail By Available Employee when you're trying to fill open shifts and need to reach employees who aren't already scheduled.

This tool filters your employee pool to show only those who are available — meaning they don't have a conflicting schedule for the time frame you select.

To send a message:
1. Go to Tools → Mail By Available Employee
2. Set your filters — branch, date range, and role if applicable
3. Review the list of available employees
4. Select the ones you want to contact, or select all
5. Compose your message and click Send

This is your go-to tool when you're short-staffed and need to reach out to employees who could potentially pick up a shift.

slides.instructions: Tools → Mail By Job. Show the job search field. Enter a job number. Show the list of scheduled employees populating automatically. Compose a message and click Send.
slides.text:
Use Mail By Job when you need to reach everyone already scheduled on a specific job at once.

To send a message:
1. Go to Tools → Mail By Job
2. Enter the Job Number or search for the job
3. The system automatically pulls all employees scheduled for that job
4. Compose your subject and message
5. Click Send — all scheduled employees receive the message simultaneously

Common uses:
• Last-minute schedule changes or location updates before an event
• Sending reminders about what to wear, where to park, or what time to arrive
• Post-event communications to everyone who worked a specific job

This is the fastest way to get a message out to your entire event crew at once.

Not sure which Mail By option to use? Here's a quick guide:

"I need to reach a specific employee or a few people by name."
→ Mail By Employee

"I have open shifts and need to find employees who aren't scheduled yet."
→ Mail By Available Employee

"I need to send the same message to everyone working a specific job."
→ Mail By Job

A few things to keep in mind:
• Messages go to the email address on file in WISH — if an employee's email is outdated, they won't receive it
• These are one-way communications — employees cannot reply through WISH
• If an employee hasn't received a message you sent, verify their email address in their employee record before resending

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti animation
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed the Mail By module.

You now know how to use all three Mail By tools to communicate with employees through WISH — whether you're reaching a specific person, filling open shifts, or sending an update to an entire job crew.

If an employee isn't receiving your messages, the first thing to check is their email address in their WISH record.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series. 
WISH is ProtaTECH’s all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.  
In this module, we’ll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.
In this module, you will learn how to configure and manage electronic sign-in and sign-out for events.

slides.instructions: Time Clock → Sign-In Config. Select Event, Venue, Job. Select shifts. Click Add to Shift List. Click Go to Sign-In.
slides.text:
Go to Time Clock → Sign-In Config. Do not use the Sign-In or Sign-Out pages directly — those show logs. Use Sign-In Config to set up your active session.

Select your event, and your venue and job will auto-populate. Select the shifts you want, click Add to Shift List, then click Go to Sign-In.

The late sign-in parameter defaults to 15 minutes — the CSC standard. Do not change this unless instructed. The 15-minute interval means sign-in times snap to the nearest quarter hour.

slides.instructions: Sign-in screen. Scan employee ID. Show auto-capture. Show late warning popup. Click Pause. Click Correction tab. Update time. Click OK.
slides.text:
In the sign-in window, employees can scan their ID badge or you can type the number manually.

If an employee is on time, they are signed in immediately. If they are late, a warning appears. Click Pause to freeze the screen and give yourself time to act. Click the Correction tab and update the time before clicking OK. (Not everyone will have the permission to correct times, check with your manager before doing so)

If an employee does not know their ID number, use the employee search link on the sign-in screen.

You can sign in employees up to two hours before their shift — they will receive their scheduled time, not the early clock time.

slides.instructions: Time Clock → Sign-In/Sign-Out Config. Show Sign-Out tab. Show 2-hour minimum parameter. Show correction process.
slides.text:
Sign-out works the same as sign-in. There is a 2-hour minimum parameter — if you try to sign someone out before they have worked two hours, the system sets the sign-out time to give them at least that minimum.

If you need to sign someone out early, use the Correction tab to adjust the time.

At the end of the event, match your sign-out count to your sign-in count as closely as possible. Click the numbers in the shift columns to see exactly who is signed in, who is signed out, and their times.

WISH keeps four timestamps per employee: scan time in, adjusted time in, scan time out, and adjusted time out.

The scan time is the actual clock time of processing. The adjusted time is the rounded result based on the 15-minute interval — or what you manually corrected it to.

A large gap between scan time and adjusted time is a flag that warrants investigation. These timestamps are visible to anyone with sign-in access.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed this portion of the WISH training. WISH is here to make workforce management easier, and you're now ready to use it with confidence.

Depending on your role and access level, there may be additional training videos. Each video provides the knowledge needed for your tools.

Thank you for taking the time to learn how WISH supports staff management.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), refer to Quick Reference Guide, contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series. 
WISH is ProtaTECH’s all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.  
In this module, we’ll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.
In this module, you will learn how to review, adjust, and approve employee hours after an event.

slides.instructions: Screen recording: Payroll → Manage Process Hours. Select event. Show job list. Click job/shift. Show sign-in and sign-out columns.
slides.text:
Go to Payroll → Manage Process Hours. Select your event from the dropdown.
You will see all jobs for that event. Click into a job to expand the shifts. For each shift, you will see the min, max, scheduled count, and sign-in count — giving you an at-a-glance view of what happened.

The sign-in count reflects what was captured electronically. This is your starting point for approving hours.

slides.instructions: Open shift. Show sign-in and sign-out buttons. Click Sign-In button — show times populating. Click Sign-Out. Click Approve Hours. Show green sunburst icons.
slides.text:
Open a shift role. If electronic sign-ins were completed, those times are already populated for each employee.

Review the times. If everything looks correct, click Approve Hours — this is your save button and finalizes the hours.

After approving, you will see green sunburst icons next to completed shifts. These let you track where you are: green means done, blue link means not yet processed.

For paper sign-in sheets, use the Sign-In and Sign-Out buttons to populate all employees with the scheduled time, then use the tab key to manually adjust individual times.

slides.instructions: Show Autofill Sign-In and Autofill Sign-Out buttons. Click both. Click Approve Hours. Back button. Move to next shift.
slides.text:
For jobs where no electronic sign-ins were done and you want to give everyone the full scheduled time, use Autofill Sign-In and Autofill Sign-Out.

These buttons instantly populate every employee with the shift's scheduled start and end time. Click Approve Hours to finalize.

This is the fastest way to process hours for clean sessions where you are confident everyone worked their full shift.

If an employee was scheduled but did not show up, they will appear with no times. You cannot approve hours while any employee has blank times.

For employees who did not show up, check the NS — No Show — checkbox next to their name. Once all no-shows are marked, you can proceed with approving hours for the rest of the shift.

Handle this carefully. A no-show mark affects payroll and the employee's record. Make sure it is accurate before saving.

When manually entering times, you will also see fields for:

Override Rate — If an employee worked a position different from their standard role and should be paid a different rate, enter it here. The system applies the higher of the two rates.

Per Diem — If you provided the employee with additional payment outside their hourly rate (such as a pay card), enter that amount here so payroll can account for it.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed this portion of the WISH training. WISH is here to make workforce management easier, and you're now ready to use it with confidence.

Depending on your role and access level, there may be additional training videos. Each video provides the knowledge needed for your tools.

Thank you for taking the time to learn how WISH supports staff management.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), refer to Quick Reference Guide, contact WISH Administrator for support.

For advanced permissions contact support-wish@csc-usa.com or call 888-791-5150.

slides.instructions: ProtaTECH logo, series title
slides.text:
Welcome to the WISH, Workforce Information Systems Hosted, training series. 
WISH is ProtaTECH’s all-in-one workforce application which supports the entire employee lifecycle—from hiring and staff management to timesheets and payroll—all within one integrated system.  
In this module, we’ll focus on the portion of WISH relevant to your role and access level.
In this module, you will learn how to generate and manage client invoices in WISH.

Before you can bill a client, two things must be true:

1. All hours for the event must be approved in Manual Process Hours. WISH will not generate a bill for a job where hours are still pending.

2. A contract must exist for the client you are billing. If no contract is in the system, the billing screens will not populate the client information.

Always complete payroll processing before billing. The order is: process hours → approve hours → then bill.

slides.instructions: Billing → Check Bill for Job. Click View Unbilled. Show report with Hours Processed column showing Yes/No.
slides.text:
Go to Billing → Check Bill for Job. Click View Unbilled to see everything that has not been invoiced yet.

The report shows a Hours Processed column. If it shows Yes, the job is ready to bill. If it shows No, go back to Manage Process Hours and complete the approval for that job first.

slides.instructions: Billing → Manage Billing Process. Select event. Show client and contract auto-populating. Hit Search. Select all days. Click Get Invoice Data.
slides.text:
Go to Billing → Manage Billing Process. Select your event — your client and contract will auto-populate.

Click Search to pull up all days worked for that event. Select the days you want to bill for. Choose your calculation method — in most cases this is Calculate Regular Hours.

Click Get Invoice Data. WISH will calculate the bill from the sign-in and sign-out data.

slides.instructions: Walk through Sign-In/Sign-Out tab, Billing Tax tab, Summary tab, Billable Items tab, Invoice Setup tab.
slides.text:
After pulling invoice data, review the tabs:

Sign-In/Sign-Out — Adjust times, change roles, or update rates for employees who worked different positions. Use the change fields at the top to apply changes to multiple employees at once.

Billing Tax — Apply tax rates to specific roles if applicable.

Summary — Clean overview of all staff used across roles and job days.

Billable Items — Add additional charges to the client: radios, carts, jackets, wristbands, etc.

Invoice Setup — Set the Attention To field (the client contact who should receive the invoice). This is required before generating.

slides.instructions: Click Generate Invoice. Select Summary or Detailed. PDF opens. Show invoice number, client info, remit-to, role breakdown, totals.
slides.text:
Select your invoice type:
• Summary — Clean totals by role. Most clients prefer this.
• Detailed — Full breakdown showing each employee's times, regular and overtime hours.
• Summary with Cover and Sign-In Sheets — Includes everything.

Click Generate Invoice. Your invoice opens as a PDF. Copy the invoice number.

To make changes, use the invoice reference dropdown — your most recent invoice appears at the top. Edit, go back to Invoice Setup, then Generate Invoice again. Each revision gets a point suffix (.01, .02, etc.) so every version is tracked.

To print any previous invoice: Billing → Print Invoice, select your event, choose the invoice.

slides.instructions: Records → Manage Contract. Search for client. Open contract. Show contract info, rates, and billing roles.
slides.text:
Client contracts are managed under Records → Manage Contract. If a contract does not exist, you will need to create one before billing is possible.

Inside a contract you will find:
• Contract dates and payment terms — standard is net 30 days with 150% late fee
• Bill rates by role — what you charge the client per hour for each position
• Tax rates — set appropriately for your location
• Contract document — upload the PDF directly into WISH

To update a rate, deactivate the current rate and enter the new one. If a role is missing, add it here with its bill rate.

slides.instructions: Checkmark/confetti
slides.text:
Congratulations! You've completed this portion of the WISH training. WISH is here to make workforce management easier, and you're now ready to use it with confidence.

Depending on your role and access level, there may be additional training videos. Each video provides the knowledge needed for your tools.

Thank you for taking the time to learn how WISH supports staff management.

slides.instructions: Arrow/progress bar
slides.text:
Proceed to next module (if applicable), refer to Quick Reference Guide, contact WISH Administrator for support.
